summary: A vivid audience FOB of an ’82 Portland show with crystal‑clear crowd energy and a second set that shines on Touch of Grey, Women Are Smarter Than Me, High Time and a legendary Other One.
review: The recording is a solid front‑of‑house (FOB) audience capture that conveys the room’s buzz while remaining clear enough for detailed listening. Musically the concert is highlighted by an incandescent second set – Touch of Grey’s delicate lick, a soaring Women Are Smarter Than Me, one of the best High Time performances ever, and a monster Other One – making it a standout 1982 show.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and praise both the performance (especially the second set) and the audience recording quality; occasional minor sound complaints do not outweigh the overall excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Front‑of‑house audience tape with clear crowd presence, generally crisp instrument balance; a few reviewers note occasional muffled spots or that it isn’t studio‑level soundboard quality.

standout_songs: Goin' Down the Road Feeling Bad
show_quality.setlist_flow: An energetic first set with high‑time jams leads into a second set that flows seamlessly through Touch of Grey → Women Are Smarter Than Me → High Time → Estimated > Eyes → Spanish Jam → The Other One → Goin' Down the Road Feeling Bad → Morning Dew → Sugar Magnolia, highlighted by Phil’s space bomb and crowd‑driven excitement.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as “on fire” with a memorable high‑pitched lick on Touch of Grey; guitar tone praised throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Very active during the Heineken years, delivering thunderous bass runs and an explosive space bomb that got the crowd chanting his name.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s 1982 country‑western piano sound noted; although his mic was missing for part of the show, his playing still shines.
